refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model to reflect current equipment state, environmental conditions, and operational events with a p95 latency not exceeding the customer-defined target (baseline 1.0 s) across all supported update scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-007 requires clarification of scope (what constitutes 'worksite model'), measurement basis (p95 latency across which scenarios), and customer-specific acceptance criteria. The refined FR explicitly bounds the latency requirement to a statistical percentile and acknowledges customer tailoring, critical for high-safety-criticality systems where deterministic guarantees matter.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrument worksite model update pipeline with end-to-end latency telemetry; measure p95 latency across representative scenarios (equipment state change, sensor event, tele-operator command, safety alert); compare against customer acceptance criteria; validate under nominal and degraded network conditions.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Capture and transmit equipment state changes (position, orientation, operational mode) with minimal local processing latency.
FR-NGHE-007.2: Receive and integrate transmitted state updates into the worksite model with bounded processing and storage latency.
FR-NGHE-007.3: Distribute updated worksite model to authorized consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Services) within latency budget.
FR-NGHE-007.4: Prioritize safety-critical updates (SafetyAlert events) to meet stricter latency targets than routine telemetry.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized worksite model server with synchronous request-response updates; all clients poll or subscribe via REST/gRPC.
DP-B: Distributed event-driven architecture with publish-subscribe messaging (MQTT/Kafka); worksite model replicated across edge nodes; safety events prioritized via separate high-priority queue.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: safety-critical updates via dedicated low-latency SafetyAlert channel; routine telemetry via standard Telemetry interface with adaptive batching; worksite model maintained locally on Vehicle and centrally on NGHE Solution with eventual consistency.
DP-D: Real-time middleware (e.g., DDS or time-triggered protocol) with deterministic scheduling; all updates synchronized to global clock; worksite model updated at fixed intervals.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid event-driven architecture with dual-path update strategy—safety-critical updates via dedicated low-latency SafetyAlert channel with strict p95 ≤ 200 ms; routine telemetry via Telemetry interface with adaptive batching and p95 ≤ 1.0 s; worksite model maintained locally on Vehicle (for real-time control) and centrally on NGHE Solution (for coordination and visualization); consistency reconciliation via periodic sync and conflict resolution.

potential_sources: Coupling between Vehicle state capture and NGHE Solution model update: mitigated by asynchronous messaging and local caching.
Coupling between SafetyAlert priority path and Telemetry routine path: mitigated by separate channels and queue prioritization.
Coupling between edge (Vehicle) and central (NGHE Solution) model replicas: mitigated by eventual consistency model and explicit sync protocol.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C decouples safety-critical latency from routine telemetry latency, reducing coupling complexity. Dual-path design allows independent optimization of each path. Eventual consistency model is acceptable for non-safety worksite state; safety state is always synchronized via dedicated channel.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network link supports dual-channel prioritization (SafetyAlert high-priority, Telemetry standard-priority).
Assumption: Vehicle and NGHE Solution can tolerate temporary model divergence for non-safety state.
Risk: If Network Link does not support QoS/prioritization, SafetyAlert latency may not meet 200 ms target.
Risk: Conflict resolution logic for model divergence may introduce additional latency if not carefully designed.
Risk: Operator confusion if Vehicle local model and central model diverge visibly; requires clear UI/UX design.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Confirm with Network Link owner that SafetyAlert channel supports strict priority queuing and latency SLA. Define explicit conflict resolution policy (e.g., 'central model is source of truth for non-safety state; vehicle model is advisory'). Specify maximum acceptable divergence window (e.g., 5 s) before forced reconciliation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Central worksite model repository, update orchestration logic, model distribution to authorized consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Services), and consistency management. Includes worksite model database, update ingestion service, and distribution service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives routine equipment state updates (position, orientation, operational mode, sensor readings) from Vehicle; supports adaptive batching and standard-priority queuing; target p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical events (e.g., collision risk, operator override, emergency stop) from Vehicle and Safety Services; high-priority queue; target p95 latency ≤ 200 ms.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Distributes updated worksite model and operational directives to Vehicle and Teleoperation module; includes model snapshots and delta updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying transport for all interfaces; NGHE Solution depends on Network Link to enforce QoS/prioritization for SafetyAlert vs. Telemetry.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of central worksite model, update orchestration, and distribution logic. High containment: all core DP-C logic (model storage, ingestion, distribution, consistency management) resides here. Low diffusion: only external dependencies are on Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, and Network Link interfaces.

Vehicle owns local worksite model replica and state capture, but does not own central model or distribution logic. High diffusion: Vehicle would need to replicate model update logic, distribution logic, and consistency management—violating single responsibility. Not recommended as primary IM.

Teleoperation is a consumer of worksite model updates, not the owner. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation would create circular dependency (Teleoperation depends on model updates, but would own the model). Not recommended.

Safety Services owns SafetyAlert generation and safety policy enforcement, but not the central worksite model. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would require Safety Services to manage routine telemetry and model distribution—outside its safety-focused scope. Not recommended as primary IM.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the single, cohesive owner of the central worksite model and update orchestration logic. Vehicle owns local model replica and state capture (sub-FR-NGHE-007.1); Safety Services owns SafetyAlert generation and prioritization (sub-FR-NGHE-007.4); Teleoperation and Site Manager are consumers. This assignment minimizes diffusion and aligns with system architecture (NGHE Solution as central coordinator).
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes overloaded with model update processing, latency SLA may be violated. Mitigation: design update ingestion service with horizontal scalability (e.g., message queue workers, load balancing).

Risk: Network Link failure or QoS degradation could violate SafetyAlert latency target. Mitigation: define fallback behavior (e.g., local safety shutdown on Vehicle if SafetyAlert not received within timeout).

Risk: Model divergence between Vehicle and NGHE Solution could cause operator confusion or safety issues. Mitigation: implement explicit conflict resolution policy and periodic reconciliation; design UI to clearly indicate model freshness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution has sufficient compute and network capacity to handle peak update rates (e.g., 100 Hz from Vehicle, 10 Hz from multiple tele-operators). Define SLA for model update ingestion service (e.g., 'process 99% of updates within 50 ms'). Specify fallback behavior if Network Link QoS is unavailable. Document conflict resolution policy in design specification.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid dual-path architecture) directly addresses FR-NGHE-007 by separating safety-critical updates (SafetyAlert, p95 ≤ 200 ms) from routine telemetry (Telemetry, p95 ≤ 1.0 s), enabling independent optimization of each path. Dual-path design is justified by high safety criticality and allows worksite model to be updated within customer-required timeliness. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of central model and orchestration logic. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services have clear, non-overlapping responsibilities.
Interface clarity: Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, and Network Link are well-defined and align with DP-C architecture.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score 0.95 indicates strong alignment. All core DP-C logic (model storage, ingestion, distribution, consistency management) is contained within NGHE Solution. External dependencies are limited to four well-defined interfaces. No significant diffusion of DP-C logic across multiple modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: instrument worksite model update pipeline with end-to-end latency telemetry; measure p95 latency across representative scenarios; validate under nominal and degraded network conditions. NGHE Solution provides clear measurement points (ingestion timestamp, distribution timestamp).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns central model and update orchestration; Vehicle owns local model and state capture; Safety Services owns SafetyAlert generation; Teleoperation and Site Manager are consumers. Sub-FRs are allocated to appropriate modules.

recommended_revisions: Confirm Network Link QoS/prioritization capability before finalizing DP-C. If not available, revise DP to DP-D (time-triggered deterministic protocol) or add dedicated SafetyAlert hardware channel.
Define explicit conflict resolution policy for model divergence (e.g., 'central model is source of truth; vehicle model is advisory; reconcile every 5 s').
Specify fallback behavior if SafetyAlert latency SLA is violated (e.g., 'Vehicle initiates local safety shutdown if SafetyAlert not received within 500 ms').
Document model update ingestion service SLA (e.g., 'process 99% of updates within 50 ms') and design for horizontal scalability.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-007 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (state capture, model integration, distribution, safety prioritization). DP-C is sound and well-justified, but requires further refinement at the next level to address sub-FR-specific DPs, IMs, and verification strategies. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but sub-FRs require allocation to Vehicle, Safety Services, and Teleoperation. Proceeding to next decomposition level is necessary to ensure complete coverage and traceability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Capture and transmit equipment state changes (position, orientation, operational mode) with minimal local processing latency (target p95 ≤ 100 ms from sensor to Telemetry interface).
FR-NGHE-007.2: Receive and integrate transmitted state updates into the worksite model with bounded processing and storage latency (target p95 ≤ 500 ms from Telemetry interface to model update).

FR-NGHE-007.3: Distribute updated worksite model to authorized consumers (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Services) within latency budget (target p95 ≤ 300 ms from model update to Tasking interface).

FR-NGHE-007.4: Prioritize safety-critical updates (SafetyAlert events) to meet stricter latency targets than routine telemetry (target p95 ≤ 200 ms from SafetyAlert generation to Safety Services receipt).

assumptions: Network Link supports QoS/prioritization (SafetyAlert high-priority, Telemetry standard-priority).
Vehicle and NGHE Solution can tolerate temporary model divergence for non-safety state (eventual consistency model is acceptable).
Customer-defined latency target is 1.0 s for routine telemetry; safety-critical updates have stricter target (200 ms assumed based on high safety criticality).
Worksite model includes equipment state, environmental conditions, and operational events; definition is stable and understood by stakeholders.
NGHE Solution has sufficient compute and network capacity to handle peak update rates.
open_risks: Network Link QoS/prioritization may not be available; if so, DP-C may not meet SafetyAlert latency target.
Model divergence between Vehicle and NGHE Solution could cause operator confusion or safety issues if conflict resolution policy is not clearly defined and implemented.
NGHE Solution overload could violate latency SLA if update ingestion service is not designed for horizontal scalability.
Fallback behavior if SafetyAlert latency SLA is violated is not yet defined; could impact safety.
evidence_needed: Network Link specification confirming QoS/prioritization capability and latency SLA.
NGHE Solution capacity analysis (compute, memory, network bandwidth) for peak update rates.
Conflict resolution policy specification and implementation design.
Fallback behavior specification (e.g., local safety shutdown on Vehicle if SafetyAlert timeout).
End-to-end latency measurement plan and test scenarios.
Customer acceptance criteria confirmation (latency target, update scenarios, measurement methodology).
